steps.n: Basic negative particle that tells us something is not happening or is not true. In this ayah, it negates the oath/swearing that follows.
Added fatha mark on ل to show how it's pronounced
Changed alif to alif madd (آ) to show it's pronounced longer. This is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the past tense form
Changed vowels to create present tense pattern with sukun, kasra, and damma
Added أُ at beginning to show 'I' am doing the action. This verb is negated by لَآ before it, making it mean 'I do not swear'
Connected the letters with vowel marks. This word is what Allah swears by in the phrase 'I swear by the Day of Resurrection.'
Added بِ to mean 'by' for swearing and ِ at the end because it's connected to the word 'resurrection' after it
The middle root letter 'و' changes to 'ي' because it's between two vowels
Changed the root letters into a simple noun meaning 'people/folk' by adding vowels
The middle letter و changes to ي and an alif is added after it to show the abstract concept
Added ة to specify one instance of rising/resurrection
Added ال to make it specific and definite

steps.m: the resurrection (in relation to يَوْمِ - day of)
steps.n: Changed alif to hamzat wasl, combined alif with yaa into ٰ, and added kasra at end to show connection to يَوْمِ (day of)
This is a negative particle that has no root - it's a basic word used to negate things
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer. This doesn't change the meaning, just the pronunciation.
The و connects this negation to what came before it in the text, adding the meaning of 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to first person present tense by adding أُ at start and changing middle vowel
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make it a noun meaning soul or self
Added ال (the) at the start to make it specific, and kasra ( ِ ) at the end because it comes after the word بِ. The double letter (shadda) on ن is part of adding ال.

steps.m: by the soul/self (object of أُقْسِمُ - I swear)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to mean 'by'. This word is part of an oath phrase 'I swear by the soul' where أُقْسِمُ means 'I swear'.

steps.m: to blame or find fault with something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
The shadda (doubled letter) and alif show this describes someone who does this action intensively or repeatedly
Adding ة makes this word feminine

steps.m: the (female) who blames a lot (describing ٱلنَّفْسِ - the soul)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ِ because it describes the word 'soul' (ٱلنَّفْسِ) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Adding يَ at the start changes it from past tense (he thought) to present tense (he thinks)
Adding أَ at the start makes it a question. This word is questioning what man (ٱلْإِنسٰنُ) thinks.
Root أنس meaning 'familiarity' becomes إِنس meaning 'mankind' - like how humans are social/familiar with each other
Adding ان to إِنس creates the word for an individual human being - like saying 'a person' instead of just 'people'
Adding ال makes it refer to humans as a specific category rather than any human

steps.m: the human being (subject of يَحْسَبُ/does he think)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is doing the action of thinking (يَحْسَبُ) in the verse
Starting with the first part of the compound particle which shows something is going to happen
Added لَ (not) to negate what comes after

steps.m: that not (negates نَّجْمَعَ - we will gather)
steps.n: Added ن to أَلَ to make أَلَّن which means 'that not' - it tells us something won't happen in the future. Here it's saying 'we will not gather'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb
The ن prefix shows 'we' are doing the action. The sukun (ْ) on ج helps with pronunciation. This word refers to Allah gathering the bones mentioned after this word (عِظَامَهُۥ)
The shadda (ّ) on ن comes from merging with the ن at the end of the previous word (أَلَّن)
Root meaning 'large/great' becomes the noun 'bone' by adding vowels - a fat-ha (َ) and sukoon (ْ)
Changes vowel pattern and adds alif to make broken plural form
Added هُۥ at the end to show possession (his) and َ to show this word is the object being collected
This is a response particle used specifically to contradict a negative statement. In this ayah, it's responding to the previous verses that questioned whether Allah can reconstruct human fingertips.

steps.m: yes indeed! (responding to doubt about Allah's power)
steps.n: The ى at the end makes this a stronger 'yes!' used specifically to respond to negative questions or doubts
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from past tense 'he had power' to a describing word meaning 'having power' - like changing 'ran' to 'running'

steps.m: those who are capable (of doing something)
steps.n: Makes the word plural to show multiple people who have power or ability. This word connects to أَن نُّسَوِّىَ (to arrange) to show what they are capable of doing.
Basic form indicating the concept of being high or above
Adding ى changes this from a concept into a preposition that connects words. Here it connects to the verb نُّسَوِّىَ (we will make equal/arrange)
The small alif (ٰ) shows this is a long vowel sound
The small vertical line (ٓ) shows the vowel is stretched when reading

steps.m: that (connects to نُّسَوِّىَ - we will arrange)
steps.n: Basic particle that introduces what will happen - in this case, it connects to the next word نُّسَوِّىَ (we will arrange) to explain what Allah is capable of doing
Basic meaning of equality or straightness
Added vowels and doubled middle letter to make it mean 'he made/arranged'
Added نُ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action, and changed vowels to show present tense

steps.m: we make equal or arrange (object of أَن)
steps.n: Added emphasis mark (shadda) on first letter and 'ah' sound at end because it follows the word 'that' (أَن) in the verse
Root related to separation and distinctness
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he separated'
Changed from verb 'separate' to noun 'fingertips' by adding alif
Added 'his' by attaching هُ to the end

steps.m: his fingertips (being acted upon by نُّسَوِّىَ)
steps.n: Added 'his' to show whose fingertips. This word is being acted upon by the verb نُّسَوِّىَ (We will arrange/make even)

steps.m: No! Rather... (introduces what follows in يُرِيدُ)
steps.n: This is a particle that means 'rather' or 'but actually'. In this ayah, it's saying 'No! Rather...' to introduce the fact that humans want to continue sinning in their future, contradicting what was discussed before.
Basic past tense verb showing someone wanted something

Added يُ at the beginning to show present tense. The و changed to ي because it's between two vowels. This word shows what the human wants to do - which is to sin/transgress (لِيَفْجُرَ) in what's ahead of him.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others

steps.n: Adds إِنْ and ان to the root to make a noun meaning 'human' - humans are social beings

steps.m: the human being (subject of يُرِيدُ - wants)
steps.n: Adds ال to make it specific and ُ to show it's doing the action in يُرِيدُ (wants)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make past tense verb

steps.m: he breaks through boundaries (subjunctive because of لِ)
steps.n: Changed from past to present tense and got a different vowel at the end because of لِ (to)

steps.m: so that he may break through boundaries (connected to يُرِيدُ - he wants)
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why 'man wants' (يُرِيدُ) from earlier in the ayah

steps.m: to be in front, to lead the way

steps.n: The ا makes it into a word meaning a place/direction
The fatha at the end makes it work like 'in front' in a sentence
Added هُۥ at the end to show it means 'his' or 'of him'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun after first root letter, and ُ at end. This verb asks about 'the Day of Resurrection' in this ayah.
This is the base question word meaning 'which'
Adding ان to أيّ makes it specifically ask 'when' about the future, like asking 'at what time will something happen?'

steps.m: when (asking about the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this is asking about a specific future event. In this ayah, it's asking about the Day of Judgment (يَوْمُ ٱلْقِيٰمَةِ) that comes after it.
Connected the letters together with vowel marks to make it a noun. This word is possessed by القيامة (Resurrection) to form 'Day of Resurrection'.
Adds ُ at the end because this word is the subject (doer) in the sentence

steps.m: to rise, to stand up straight

steps.n: Changed يَا to يٰ (alif written above) for proper Quranic spelling
Added ة to make it mean a specific instance of rising - the ultimate rising on Judgment Day
Adding definite article makes the noun definite

steps.m: of the resurrection (modifies يَوْمُ - the Day)
steps.n: Added ِ to show this describes يَوْمُ (the Day) in the phrase 'Day of Resurrection'
This is a time word that means 'when' something happens. In this ayah, it introduces what happens when بَرِقَ ٱلْبَصَرُ (the vision becomes dazzled)
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this with what happened before, showing that this happens next in a sequence of events

steps.m: it became dazzled/stunned (describes ٱلْبَصَرُ - the vision)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to make a past tense verb. In this ayah, this word describes how the vision/sight (ٱلْبَصَرُ) becomes dazzled or stunned in fear.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to create the noun meaning 'sight'
Added the ُ (damma) ending to show this is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: the sight (subject of بَرِقَ - became dazzled)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it definite. This word is the subject of بَرِقَ (became dazzled) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is about the moon (ٱلْقَمَرُ) disappearing.
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect it to what came before it
Added fatha vowels to make the noun meaning 'moon' - the thing that shines brightly at night
Added 'the' at the beginning and ُ at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in خَسَفَ (darkened)

steps.m: to gather, collect, or bring things together

steps.n: Changed to passive form to show that the sun and moon were gathered by someone else. This matches with the next words in the ayah (ٱلشَّمْسُ وَٱلْقَمَرُ) which tell us what was gathered.
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this with previous statements. This word introduces what happens to the sun and moon.
Changed from action of shining to the thing that shines (sun)
The sun gets a stronger 'sh' sound at the start because of the word before it
Added 'the' to make it specific
Added ُ to show this is the subject of جُمِعَ (was gathered)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun. This word is one of the subjects of جُمِعَ (was gathered) along with الشمس (the sun)

steps.m: the moon (subject of جُمِعَ - was gathered)
steps.n: Added 'al' (ال) at the beginning to make it 'the moon'

steps.m: and the moon (subject of جُمِعَ - was gathered)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the start and ُ (damma) at the end to show this is a subject of the verb جُمِعَ (was gathered)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense form

steps.m: he says/is saying (describing what الإنسان/man does)
steps.n: Added يَ at start to show present tense. The middle و becomes a long vowel (ُو) instead of staying as وَ. Added ُ at the end to show this is the doer of the action. This word describes what الإنسان (man) does in this ayah.
Forms the word for 'human' - humans are naturally social beings, which connects to the root meaning of being social/friendly

steps.m: the human being (subject of يَقُولُ - he says)
steps.n: ٱلْ makes it specific (like 'the' in English). The ُ shows this human is doing the action of saying (يَقُولُ) in this verse
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added fatha at the end to show this word is affected by something

steps.m: on that day (when this is happening)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ to specify 'that particular day' being discussed in the ayah

steps.m: where? (asking about the escape mentioned in ٱلْمَفَرُّ)
steps.n: This is a question word asking about location. In this ayah, the human is frantically asking 'Where is the escape?' on the Day of Judgment.
Added مَ at start to show where/when something happens, and doubled the last letter to show intensity

steps.m: the escape (subject of أَيْنَ - where is)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ُ to show it's what we're asking about with 'where'. This word is being asked about by أَيْنَ (where) in this ayah.
This is an emphatic particle used to strongly negate or reject what came before it. It's stronger than just saying 'no'.
Adding shadda (doubling mark) makes the word stronger, like saying 'absolutely' instead of just 'no'

steps.m: No indeed! By no means! (used for strong denial or rejection)
steps.n: Adding alif at the end makes this a special word that strongly rejects what came before. In this ayah, it's rejecting the idea that there might be refuge or escape.
This is the basic negative particle in Arabic, like saying 'no' or 'not'
Added fatha mark to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: he sought refuge (in response to كَلَّا لَا - 'No!')
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb. This word comes after كَلَّا لَا ('No!') to show the response of seeking refuge.
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to رَبِّكَ (your Lord) to show where the final destination is.
Added an alif that looks like a little stick (ىٰ) at the end to show how to pronounce it correctly when continuing to the next word

steps.m: to care for, nurture, and help grow
lord (one who takes complete care of others)
steps.n: The doubled letter ّ in the middle makes the meaning stronger, showing complete and perfect care
The ِ shows this word belongs to or is connected to something (in this case, it will be connected to 'you')
The كَ at the end means 'your' when speaking to one male. This word follows إِلَىٰ (to) in the ayah, showing movement or direction towards your Lord.
Added fatha at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it

steps.m: on that day (referring to the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ which means 'that' or 'then', making it refer specifically to the Day of Judgment being discussed in this surah
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show basic past tense form
The مست prefix changes it from a verb (settling) to a noun (place of settling)
The ال makes it definite, referring to a specific destination
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows this is done thoroughly or with emphasis
Changed to passive voice by adding yu- prefix and -u ending. Shows the action is being done to someone.

steps.m: they will be thoroughly informed (refers to الإنسان)
steps.n: Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding a small circle above alif at the end. The hamza changed shape because of the u-sound before it.
Root letters got vowels to make past tense 'he' form
From verb 'to be social' to noun meaning 'human' - showing how humans are social beings by nature
Added 'the' to make it definite

steps.m: the human being (subject of يُنَبَّؤُا۟ - will be informed)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this is the subject who will be informed
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being described by what comes after it

steps.m: on that day (when the human is informed of their deeds)
steps.n: Adding ئِذٍ makes it mean 'at that specific time/on that specific day'. This word connects to يُنَبَّؤُا۟ (is informed) to show when the informing happens.
Basic form of the relative pronoun meaning 'what'

steps.m: with/by what (relates to يُنَبَّؤُا - he will be informed)
steps.n: Added the preposition بِ to show how or by what means the person will be informed of their deeds
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) between root letters to make past tense 'he' form

steps.m: he sent forward (what deeds he did)
steps.n: Adding shadda makes the meaning more intense and shows deliberate action. In this ayah, it refers to the deeds that a person deliberately sent ahead for their afterlife.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled خ shows he did this action strongly or multiple times (mentioned in ayah 75:13 about what someone did in their past)

steps.m: and he delayed (intensively) (connected to قَدَّمَ - what he sent ahead)
steps.n: Added و to connect this with the previous word قَدَّمَ (sent ahead), showing contrast between what someone sent ahead and what they delayed
This is a basic word that introduces a contrast or correction to what was said before. It's like saying 'Actually...' or 'But instead...' in English.
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because it connects to the next word ٱلْإِنسٰنُ (the human). This is like adding glue to connect two words together.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others

steps.n: Root means 'to be social' - adding vowels makes it mean 'humans' since we are social creatures
Adding ان makes it refer to a single human, showing our social nature is a key characteristic
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and changed alif to alif with bar for writing style
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
This is the basic form of the word that means something being high or elevated
Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition that shows one thing is on top of or over another thing

steps.m: upon, on (preposition showing location relative to نَفْسِهِۦ)
steps.n: The small alif (ٰ) is added to show this is a special form of ى that keeps its 'aa' sound at the end. In this ayah, it shows the relationship between something and نَفْسِهِۦ (himself)
Root letters got vowels to make the common word for 'soul/self'
Added i-sound (kasra) at the end because it follows the word 'upon' (على)
Added هِۦ to show possession - 'his self'. The ۦ is just a pronunciation mark.

steps.m: to see clearly or understand deeply

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed pattern to show someone/something with this quality strongly
Added feminine ending to create a noun meaning 'clear proof' or 'evidence'. In this ayah, it refers to the evidence against oneself.
Added tanween to show this is indefinite - 'a clear evidence' rather than 'the clear evidence'
This is a conditional particle that introduces hypothetical scenarios

steps.m: and even if (connects to أَلْقَىٰ - he throws)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with what comes after it. This word introduces a hypothetical scenario about someone throwing excuses.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final ي changes to ى because it's at the end of the word.
Added أَ at the beginning to show the action is done with more force or intention. The first vowel changes to sukun.

steps.m: he threw (even though he made excuses - مَعَاذِيرَهُۥ)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ٰ) to show this is the final form. This word connects to مَعَاذِيرَهُۥ (his excuses) which comes after it in the ayah.
Basic meaning of making an excuse or giving a reason
Changed from root 'to excuse' into noun 'excuse' by adding the vowel marks U and sukoon
Changed عُذْر to مَعَاذِير by adding مَ at start and making it plural, like changing 'excuse' to 'excuses'
Added هُۥ to show possession (his) and َ to show this is the object being thrown in the phrase 'even if he throws his excuses'
This is a negative word that tells us not to do something. In this ayah, it's telling us not to move something.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows doing the action more strongly or making something else do it
Added تُ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Adding a stop mark (sukoon) at the end changes this from 'you move' to 'do not move!' when used with لَا
Basic preposition indicating accompaniment or means
Added attached pronoun هِ to show what the preposition refers to

steps.m: with/by it (masculine) (referring to the Quran in لِتَعْجَلَ بِهِۦٓ)
steps.n: Added small ۦ to clarify that the pronoun is masculine, referring to the Quran in the phrase 'to hasten with it'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the first vowel to kasra and added alif to make the word for 'tongue'
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being moved in the phrase 'Do not move your tongue'
Added كَ to show the tongue belongs to 'you'. This word is what's being moved in the phrase 'Do not move your tongue'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding تَ at the beginning shows 'you' are doing the action, and changing the vowels shows it's happening now

steps.m: so that you hurry (connected to لَا تُحَرِّكْ - do not move)
steps.n: Adding لِ shows this is the purpose or reason for the action mentioned before (not moving the tongue). It explains why something is happening.
Added kasra to show it's a preposition
Added هِ to show it refers to something masculine (in this case, referring back to the Quran)
Added small ي and elongation mark to emphasize the masculine pronoun, this is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning
Adding the shadda makes this word emphasize what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that something is definitely upon us (عَلَيْنَا).
Added sukun (ْ) before adding the pronoun suffix. This is a helper step to prepare for attaching the pronoun.
Added نَا at the end to show this applies to 'us'. This word is connected to إِنَّ (indeed) at the start of the phrase, showing what is definitely upon us.

steps.m: to gather, collect, or bring things together

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun showing the action itself. This word is being acted on by عَلَيْنَا (upon us) in this ayah, showing what is upon us to do.

steps.m: gathering it/him (object of عَلَيْنَا - upon us)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end which means 'it/him', showing what is being gathered. The َ (fatha) before it shows this word is being acted upon by عَلَيْنَا (upon us).
The root 'read/recite' was turned into a special noun by adding ء and ان to make the word for 'recitation' (Quran). Like changing 'read' into 'reading'
Added هُ at the end to show possession. This word is connected to جَمْعَهُ (gathering it) in the ayah, showing what Allah is gathering/preserving.
Added وَ (and) at the start to connect this word to جَمْعَهُ (gathering it), showing that Allah is responsible for both gathering and preserving the Quran's recitation.

steps.m: when (followed by قَرَأْنٰهُ - We recite it)
steps.n: This is a time word that shows when something happens. Here it introduces what happens when the Quran is recited.

steps.m: so when/then when (followed by قَرَأْنٰهُ - We recite it)
steps.n: The فَ at the beginning shows this happens after something else. In this ayah, it connects what happens after the Quran is recited.
This is the basic meaning of reading or reciting something
Adding vowel marks turns the root into a past tense verb meaning 'he read'
Added نٰ at the end to show 'we' did the action instead of 'he'

steps.m: we read/recited it aloud (referring to the Quran)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end meaning 'it', referring back to the Quran that is being read
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he followed' to 'follow!' by making it a command - added alif at start and changed the vowels
Added emphasis by doubling the middle letter with shadda

steps.m: then follow closely! (after قَرَأْنٰهُ - We have recited it)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this action follows after 'We have recited it' in the verse
This is the basic meaning of reading or reciting something
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense
Changes to pattern فُعْلان to show this is a complete collection of recited verses
Added هُۥ at the end to show possession (its) and َ to show this is the object being followed in the command 'follow it'
This is a common connecting word that links events in order. It shows that one thing happens after another, with a longer pause than وَ (and).
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning and show emphasis. This word connects to the next statement that starts with إِنَّ (indeed), showing a sequence in the explanation.
This is the basic form of the particle that shows a condition or possibility.
Adding the shadda (ّ) changes this from a word meaning 'if' to one that makes statements stronger and more certain. In this ayah, it emphasizes Allah's statement about what comes next.
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for the next addition. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
The نا at the end means 'us' - it shows this affects a group that includes the speaker. This word connects to إِنَّ (indeed) earlier in the verse.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
This pattern turns the verb into a noun meaning 'the act of explaining'. The word is in accusative case because it is the object of the preposition علي (upon).
The هُ at the end means 'his/its' - it shows who the explanation belongs to. This word is the object of علينا (upon us) in the accusative case, showing what is upon us to do.
Connected the base letters with fatha to form the basic word structure
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning

steps.m: No indeed! (used for strong denial or rejection)
steps.n: Added alif at the end to make it an emphatic particle. This word appears at the beginning of this verse to strongly reject what came before it.

This is a particle that strongly corrects or contradicts what came before. Here it emphasizes the correction of كَلَّا (No!) and introduces the real problem - that they love the immediate life too much.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The middle letter is doubled to show intensity of the love
Added تُ at the beginning to show present tense for 'you'

steps.m: you (all) intensely love (the عَاجِلَة - worldly life)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show that multiple people are doing the action. This word relates to العَاجِلَة (the worldly life) which is the object being loved.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to show the doer or state of being quick
Added feminine marker to specify this refers to the immediate/worldly life
Added the definite article and fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb 'you love' (تُحِبُّونَ) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the past tense verb
Changed to present tense addressing 'you'. This verb is acting on ٱلْءَاخِرَةَ (the Hereafter) which comes after it in the ayah.
The ون ending makes it plural, addressing multiple people

steps.m: and you all leave/abandon (the ٱلْءَاخِرَةَ - Hereafter)
steps.n: Adding conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'
Changes from basic root meaning 'after/delay' to describe something that comes later or last. Like changing 'delay' to 'delayed' in English.
Added feminine ending to make it refer to a state or concept rather than a person

steps.m: the Hereafter (object of تَذَرُونَ - you leave)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it definite and َ to show it's the object being left/abandoned in the phrase وَتَذَرُونَ ٱلْءَاخِرَةَ (and you leave the Hereafter)
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create the basic noun for 'face'
Changed vowels to damma and added و between letters to make plural form
Adds short vowels (fat-ha marks) to make it mean 'on a day' or 'at a time'

steps.m: on that day (when faces will be نَّاضِرَةٌ - radiant)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ which means 'that/then' to specify which day. This word connects to the context of the ayah about faces being radiant.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to active participle form to describe the faces that are doing the action of being radiant
Added ة to make the word feminine since it describes faces (وجوه) which is feminine in Arabic
The shadda (doubled ن) makes the word stronger/more intense in meaning, showing very bright and radiant faces

steps.m: intensely radiant (feminine, nominative case, describes وُجُوهٌ/faces)
steps.n: Added tanween dammah to show this is nominative case like وُجُوهٌ
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows direction or movement towards something
Added the alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is a common way to write this preposition. In this ayah, it shows the direction of looking - the faces are looking towards their Lord.

steps.m: master who cares for others (after إِلَىٰ)
steps.n: Root ر-ب-ب becomes رَبِّ (Lord/Master) - the word that cares for and nurtures others. The ّ (shadda) shows emphasis.

steps.m: her master who cares for her (after إِلَىٰ)
steps.n: The هَا at the end means 'her' and shows possession. The word is in the genitive case because it comes after the preposition إِلَىٰ (to).

steps.m: to look at, gaze upon, observe attentively
one who gazes intently (subject of إِلَىٰ)
steps.n: This form shows who does the action. The word connects with إِلَىٰ (towards) to show direction of gaze.
Makes the word feminine to match the subject (faces) it describes
Makes the word indefinite with tanween/nunation
Connected the root letters together to make the basic word for a single face
Changed the word pattern to show multiple faces
Added double damma to show these faces are doing something in the sentence
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action

steps.m: that day (when faces will be gloomy)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍۭ to specify 'that specific day' being discussed in the ayah. This word connects to 'باسِرَة' (gloomy) which describes the faces on that day.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to change from verb (he frowned) to noun (one who frowns)
Added ة to match the feminine word وُجُوهٌ (faces) and ٌ to show it's in the nominative case like وُجُوهٌ

steps.m: to think or believe something without being completely sure

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form
Changed to passive voice by putting damma on first letter
Combined the double letters with a shadda

steps.m: she thinks/believes (about what will happen to her)
steps.n: Added تَ at beginning to show 'she' is doing the action, and ُ at end to show it's happening now. In this ayah, it refers to souls thinking about their punishment.
This is a particle that connects two parts of the sentence. Here it connects 'she thinks' (تَظُنُّ) with 'will be done' (يُفْعَلَ), showing what she thinks will happen.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show that something receives the action instead of doing it

steps.m: to be done/made (that something will happen to it)
steps.n: Added يُ at the start to show present/future tense. The middle vowel changed to no vowel (sukun). The ending -a shows it comes after 'that' (أَن).
Added kasra (ِ) to show how the preposition is pronounced

steps.m: with/by her/it (referring to فَاقِرَةٌ - a catastrophe)
steps.n: Added هَا which refers back to فَاقِرَةٌ (a catastrophe) mentioned right after this word in the ayah

steps.m: to break or crush something severely

steps.n: The alif after the first letter turns the verb 'to break' into a noun meaning 'something that breaks really badly' - like turning 'break' into 'breaker'
Adding feminine marker ة makes it refer to a devastating event or calamity

steps.m: a crushing calamity (that will devastate them)
steps.n: The tanween (double damma) shows this is an indefinite noun, emphasizing how it's an unspecified but terrible calamity
This is a particle of emphatic negation or rejection. It's stronger than just saying 'no'
Adding shadda makes the word stronger, like saying something with more force
Adding alif at the end makes it even more emphatic, showing strong rejection

steps.m: No indeed! - used to strongly reject or deny something (introduces إِذَا بَلَغَتِ ٱلتَّرَاقِىَ)
steps.n: The long alif (آ) shows this is a complete stop. This word introduces the following phrase about 'when the soul reaches the collar bones', strongly emphasizing the seriousness of that moment.
This is the basic form of the word that indicates a point in time

Adding ا at the end makes it specifically talk about something that will definitely happen in the future or a conditional situation. Here it introduces what happens when the soul reaches the collar bones.
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: she reached (referring to الروح - the soul)
steps.n: Added تِ at the end to show this is feminine (she) instead of masculine (he). In this ayah, it's referring to الروح (the soul) reaching the collar bones at the time of death.
This forms the plural noun meaning 'collarbones'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
This is a different word - 'taraaqi' is the plural of 'tarquwa' meaning collarbone, not related to rising upward
Added ال to make it definite and shadda on ت because it comes after ال
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being reached in the phrase 'when it reaches the collarbones'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form
Changed the vowels to show this happened to something rather than something doing it

steps.m: and it was said (connected to مَنْ رَاقٍ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the words that come after it (مَنْ رَاقٍ - who is an enchanter?)

steps.m: who (asking about the person who will be رَاقٍ)
steps.n: This is a question word used to ask 'who?' about people. In this ayah, it's asking who will be the healer/charmer (رَاقٍ).

steps.m: to rise, ascend, or charm/heal (with ruqyah)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changes from verb (he rose) to noun (one who rises/ascends)
Adding vowel marks to make it a complete past tense verb

steps.m: and he thought/believed (connected to أَنَّهُ ٱلْفِرَاقُ - that it was the parting)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning to connect it to what comes after it in the verse - that it was the parting
This is a particle that connects to ظَنَّ (he thought) to introduce what was thought

steps.m: that it (referring to الْفِرَاقُ - the parting)
steps.n: Added هُ (it) to show what was thought about. Here it refers to الْفِرَاقُ (the parting) that comes after this word.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to a noun meaning the act of separation by adding alif after second root letter and changing vowels
Added the definite article 'the'. This word is the predicate (describes what is) after أَنَّهُ (that it)
Added u-sound at the end because this word completes the meaning after 'that' (أَنَّهُ)

steps.m: to join or wrap something around something else

steps.n: Added vowels and combined letters with shadda on final ف to show basic past tense form
Added اِلْتَ to show the person is doing the wrapping action to themselves (like turning/wrapping around)
Added و at beginning to connect this action with previous statement

steps.m: and she turned herself around (subject of ٱلسَّاقُ)
steps.n: Added تِ at end to show the subject is feminine (the leg/shin). This connects to the word ٱلسَّاقُ (the leg) which is feminine in Arabic.
The و changes to ا and we add َ to make it a noun for the thing that does the driving/moving forward - the leg
Added emphasis to the س with shadda

steps.m: the leg (that's being wrapped around the other leg)
steps.n: Added 'the' (ٱل) at the start and a mark (ُ) at the end to show it's the leg being talked about
Adding alif makes it past tense
Adding kasra at end shows this word is affected by a preposition
Added بِ at start to show this leg is being used as an instrument or means.
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination
Added the small alif (ىٰ) at the end to show this is a complete word that connects to what comes after it

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop to completion

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows intensity, emphasizing the complete nature of lordship
The word رَبّ (lord) changes to رَبِّ when something is added after it to show possession, like 'my lord' or 'your lord'
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to make the basic word for 'day'
Added ئِذٍ to specify 'that day' or 'at that time'. This word connects to ٱلْمَسَاقُ (the destination) in the ayah, showing when the destination will be reached.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb. The middle و becomes ا in this pattern.
Added مَ at the beginning to change it from 'driving forward' to 'the final place something is driven to'

steps.m: the final destination (where all will be driven to on the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific ('the destination') and ُ to show this is the subject being talked about in the ayah
This is the basic negation word that means 'not' or 'no'

steps.m: and then not (connects to صَدَّقَ - 'he believed')
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from and connects to what happened before. In this ayah, it connects to the verb صَدَّقَ (he believed).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: he affirmed strongly (connected to فَلَا - 'but no/not')
steps.n: Added shadda to make the meaning stronger. In this ayah, it's negated by فَلَا (but no/not) showing 'he did not strongly affirm'
This is the basic negation word that means 'not' - it negates the verb صَلَّىٰ (prayed) that comes after it

steps.m: and not (connects to فَلَا صَدَّقَ and negates صَلَّىٰ)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this negation with the previous phrase فَلَا صَدَّقَ (he did not give charity), showing these are related ideas
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
The middle letter is doubled to show doing something with extra effort or care. The و changes to ى because it's at the end of the word.
The small alif above the ى shows it should be pronounced with a long 'aa' sound at the end
This is a coordinating conjunction that introduces a contrast with what came before

steps.m: and yet/but still (connecting to كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّىٰ - he denied and turned away)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what comes after it. This creates a flowing narrative showing contrast: 'and yet/but still he denied and turned away'
Basic past tense verb pattern with fatha vowels
Adding shadda on middle letter makes the meaning stronger. This word comes after وَلٰكِن (but) to show contrast.

steps.m: to be close to or follow something

steps.n: Added vowels to the root letters to make it mean 'he followed' in the past
Added تَ at start and doubled ل to show someone actively turning themselves away
Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Added superscript alif (ٰ) as a pronunciation guide
This is a connecting word that links events in order. The shadda (double letter) makes it stronger.
The shadda (ّ) makes the meaning stronger, showing a clear sequence of events. In this ayah, it connects to the next action ذَهَبَ (he went), showing what happened next.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. In this ayah, it's followed by إِلَىٰ (to) showing where he went.
This is the basic form of the word that shows direction or destination

steps.m: to, towards (connected to أَهْلِهِۦ - his family)
steps.n: The ىٰ (alif maqsurah with small alif above) is just a writing style variation - it's pronounced the same as regular ى

steps.m: to, towards (connected to أَهْلِهِۦ - his family)
steps.n: The small mark (ٓ) above shows the word is connected in meaning to what follows it
Connected the letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make the basic noun
Adding kasra shows this word is connected to the word إِلَىٰٓ (to) that comes before it
The هِ at the end means 'his'. This word is in the genitive case because it comes after the preposition إِلَىٰٓ (to).
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added ت at beginning and doubled the middle letter to show doing the action to oneself
Added يَ at the beginning to show the action is happening now
Added ى at the end to extend the final sound
Added أ at beginning and vowels to create comparative form. In this ayah, it's a threat meaning 'woe to you' or 'you deserve punishment'
Added ىٰ (alif maqsurah with superscript alif) to show this is a special form of the word that appears at the end of phrases
This is the basic preposition that shows something is being directed towards something else
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced

steps.m: for you (masculine singular) (warning to the one who denies)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ك with fatha to show this is directed to a single male person. In this ayah, it's part of a warning to those who deny the truth.

steps.m: to be close to or in charge of something
he was close to/in charge of
steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added أ at the beginning and changed the middle vowel to make it mean 'more/most'
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from something before it
Added the small alif (ٰ) to show how to pronounce the end of the word with a long sound
This is the basic form of the word that shows something happens after something else
Added vowels and shadda to complete the word. In this ayah, it connects two warnings or threats, showing the second one (فَأَوْلَىٰٓ - then woe) comes after the first.

steps.m: to be close to or take charge of something

steps.n: Added أ at the beginning to make it mean 'more' or 'most'. The ى at the end shows it's comparing something (like saying 'better' or 'best'). Comes from the root meaning 'close' or 'in charge'.
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced as a long aa sound
Added fatha to show this is a preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'

steps.m: for/to you (connected to أَوْلَىٰ - more fitting)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ك with fatha to show this is directed to 'you'. This word connects to أَوْلَىٰ (more fitting) to mean 'more fitting for you'

steps.m: to be close to or take charge of something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added أ at start and changed middle vowel to make comparative form meaning 'more/most'
Added alif maksura at end to show this is the final form of the word

steps.m: then more/most deserving (connected to previous statement أَوْلَىٰ)
steps.n: Added فَ at start to connect with previous statement, showing this is a consequence or sequence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show present tense. The ُ at the end shows he is doing the action.

steps.m: does he think/calculate? (questioning if الْإِنسٰنُ thinks)
steps.n: Added أَ at start to make it a question. This word is questioning what الْإِنسٰنُ (mankind/human) thinks.
Root meaning 'familiar/close' becomes a noun meaning 'humans' - like how humans are social/familiar with each other
Changes from general 'humankind' to mean one specific human
Makes it specific/definite
The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) for writing style
The ُ shows this word is doing the action of thinking/assuming in the verse
This is a basic connecting word that joins two parts of a sentence, showing that the first part leads to or relates to the second part. Here it connects 'does he think' with 'he will be left'
Added fatha mark on أ to show correct pronunciation
Added vowels to make it 'he left' in the past tense
Changed vowels to show passive voice - something being done to someone rather than someone doing it

steps.m: he will be left/abandoned (object of أَن)
steps.n: Added يُ at start to show present/future tense and sukun on first root letter. This word comes after أَن (that) in the ayah, showing what man thinks will happen.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Transforms from verb 'to leave unattended' into noun meaning 'useless/neglected'

steps.m: Did...? (turns the statement into a question)
steps.n: This letter turns any statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English
Added لَ to add emphasis to the question

steps.m: Did not...? (asking about something in the past that connects to يَكُ)
steps.n: Added مْ which makes the question negative. This word connects to يَكُ (be) in the ayah to ask 'Was it not?'
The root letters that mean 'to be'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, and changed middle vowels to match present tense pattern
The word becomes shorter in jussive mood by removing the و
The root letters that mean dripping or flowing
Changed from a root meaning 'to flow' into a noun meaning 'a drop' by adding vowel marks
Added ة to specify a single instance
Added tanween to show this is the object of يَكُ (was) in the phrase 'Was he not a drop?'
This word connects نُطْفَةً (a drop) to what comes after it, showing where the drop came from. The shadda (ّ) makes the 'م' sound stronger to emphasize the connection.
The root letters with basic vowels
Makes it a past tense verb showing something flowed or was emitted
The doubled م makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic

The word changed from a verb (flowed) to a noun (semen) by adding the double dots under (tanween kasra) and the small mark on top (shadda). It connects to يُمْنَىٰ which tells us the semen is being emitted.
The root letters in present tense form
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form past tense verb
Added يُ at start and changed vowels to show passive voice (something being done to it)

steps.m: it is emitted (describing مَّنِىٍّ - seminal fluid)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ىٰ) at the end to show this word is describing how something flows or pours out
This is the basic form of the word that shows something happens after something else

steps.m: then (connects كَانَ عَلَقَةً 'was a clinging substance' to what came before)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) and shadda (ّ) to strengthen the meaning. This word connects the stages of creation, showing one stage comes after another.
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to make it pronounceable

steps.m: he was (followed by عَلَقَةً - a clinging substance)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because this is a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). This is a common change in past tense verbs.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Adding taa marbuta transforms the verb into a noun describing a thing that clings/hangs. Common pattern for creating concrete nouns from verbs.

steps.m: a blood clot (object being transformed in كَانَ)
steps.n: Adding tanween (the -an sound) shows this is the object being changed into something else
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. The 'he' refers to Allah in this context.

steps.m: and then He created (after making it عَلَقَةً/a clinging substance)
steps.n: The فَ shows this creation happened right after the previous stage mentioned in the ayah (being a clinging substance/عَلَقَةً)

steps.m: to make something balanced or complete
steps.n: This root is about making things complete or balanced
Added the shadda to show intensive action of making something complete and perfect
Added فَ to show this happened right after Allah created the blood clot (عَلَقَة)

steps.m: and then He fashioned (it) perfectly (after خَلَقَ)
steps.n: Added the alif maksura at the end to complete the word. This word comes after Allah mentions creating (خَلَقَ), showing He perfected what He created.

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is about something that happened in the past. This word is the main verb describing Allah's action in this verse.

steps.m: and then He made (referring to Allah creating from a drop two pairs)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this action follows after what was mentioned before - the creation from a drop. The فَ here shows the sequence of Allah's creative acts.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'

steps.m: from him/it (object of جَعَلَ - he made)
steps.n: Added pronoun هُ to show who/what is being referred to. This whole phrase مِنْهُ is being acted on by the verb جَعَلَ (he made) in the ayah.

steps.m: to pair or make into pairs

steps.n: Root 'pair' became 'spouse/mate' by adding vowel marks. It's the thing being 'made' in the sentence
Added dual ending to show exactly two. This word is the direct object (what is being made) in the sentence, showing what Allah made.
Added the definite article 'the' which causes the first letter to double (shown by ّ)
Adding vowel marks changes it from a root into a complete verb
These are different words - ذَكَرَ means 'he remembered' while ذَّكَر means 'male'. The shadda is not for emphasis here.
Added 'the' at the beginning and 'a' at the end to show this is the object being made in the phrase 'He made from it the male'
This form makes a feminine noun from the root
Added definite article. This word is paired with ٱلذَّكَرَ (the male) in the ayah to show contrast.
Added conjunction و
Added feminine marker ى to complete the noun
Adding vowels to make it a usable past tense verb meaning 'is not'
Added أَ at the beginning to turn the negative statement into a question. This word is questioning ذٰلِكَ (that) in this ayah.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) above the ذ
Adding لِ changes this from pointing to something near to pointing to something far

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) (subject of أَلَيْسَ)
steps.n: Adding كَ makes it a complete word meaning 'that' (pointing to something far away). It's the subject being asked about in this ayah.

steps.m: to have power or ability over something

steps.n: Added alif after first letter to show who does the action, and kasra under second letter to complete the pattern

steps.m: by/with a powerful one (connected to أَلَيْسَ - is not)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show this describes how something is done. This word is part of the question 'Is not that One by/with power...?'
This is the basic preposition form showing something is on top of or higher than something else
The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) that shows it's the same word but written differently
Added a small alif (ٓ) above to help with pronunciation when continuing to the next word

steps.m: that (connects to يُحْۦِىَ/to bring to life)
steps.n: This is a connecting particle that links the previous part of the verse to the verb that follows it. Here it connects 'Is He not able to' with 'bring the dead to life', showing what Allah is able to do.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added يُ at start to make it mean 'he causes/makes' instead of just 'he'. The word changed from 'he lived' to 'he gives life to something else'

steps.m: he gives life to (object of قٰدِرٍ - able to)
steps.n: The verb changes its ending because it comes after أن (that). The ي changes to ى because it's at the end. This verb is connected to being 'able to' give life.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added ى (alif maqsurah) at the end to change from 'death' to plural meaning 'dead people/ones'

steps.m: the dead ones (object of يُحْۦِىَ - gives life to)
steps.n: Added the definite article ٱلْ to specify these particular dead ones. This word is the object of يُحْۦِىَ (gives life to) in this ayah.
